cards.front: side order (not part of le menu)
How do you say 'to express oneself'?
How do you say 'to get married'?
How do you say 'to sit down'?
How do you say 'to meet / find each other again'?
cards.notes: Can mean to meet up again or to find oneself/each other.

cards.front: How do you say 'to enjoy / savor'?
How do you say 'to miss / pass by'?
cards.notes: Often used for missing an opportunity.

cards.front: How do you contrast emotional feeling and physical feeling/smell?
cards.back: ressentir = feel emotionally; sentir = feel physically / smell

cards.front: How do you say 'a speaker / orator'?
How do you say 'a breath / a puff'?
How do you say 'head held high'?
How do you say 'with ease / effortlessly'?
How do you say 'It can be learned'?
How do you say 'to speak up / take the floor'?
What is the French causative pattern with faire?
cards.notes: Example: faire rire Paul = to make Paul laugh.

cards.front: How do you say 'to make your voice heard'?
How do you say 'instructions / user guide'?

cards.notes: The note glosses it as inégal.

cards.front: How do you say 'to separate / detach'?
cards.notes: The note gives séparer and détacher.

cards.front: How do you say 'to start / launch oneself into something'?
How do you say 'to compete / clash'?

cards.notes: Used for something raw, exposed, or felt intensely.

cards.front: When do you use the subjunctive?
cards.back: On utilise le subjonctif pour exprimer un sentiment, un doute, une volonté, une obligation ou un jugement.

cards.front: Which mood follows a feeling like 'J'ai peur que'?
Which mood follows doubt or negative opinion?
cards.back: Je doute que + subjonctif; Je ne pense pas que + subjonctif.

cards.front: Which mood follows wishes such as 'Je veux que' or 'J'aimerais que'?
cards.back: Je veux que + subjonctif; J'aimerais que + subjonctif.

cards.front: How do you contrast certainty vs wish with venir?
cards.back: Je sais qu'il vient. / Je veux qu'il vienne.
cards.notes: Indicatif for certainty; subjonctif for volonté.

cards.front: What are the regular present subjunctive endings?
How do you form the regular present subjunctive stem?
cards.back: On prend la forme ils/elles au présent, on enlève -ent, puis on ajoute les terminaisons.

cards.front: Conjugate être in the present subjunctive.
cards.back: que je sois, que tu sois, qu'il/elle soit, que nous soyons, que vous soyez, qu'ils/elles soient

cards.front: Conjugate avoir in the present subjunctive.
cards.back: que j'aie, que tu aies, qu'il/elle ait, que nous ayons, que vous ayez, qu'ils/elles aient

cards.front: Conjugate aller in the present subjunctive.
cards.back: que j'aille, que tu ailles, qu'il/elle aille, que nous allions, que vous alliez, qu'ils/elles aillent

cards.front: Give the present subjunctive of faire, pouvoir, and vouloir for je.
cards.back: que je fasse; que je puisse; que je veuille
il faut que, bien que, pour que, afin que, avant que, à condition que, jusqu'à ce que + subjonctif

cards.front: Which connectors take the indicative in the notes?
cards.back: après que + indicatif; parce que + indicatif

cards.front: How do you say 'It's not a big deal'?
cards.back: Ce n'est pas grave. / C'est pas grave.

cards.front: How do you say 'about to + verb'?
How do you say 'however / on the other hand'?
How do you say 'it is about / this is about'?
How do you say 'to press a button'?
How do you say 'if you feel like it'?
How do you say 'to take off / start'?
How do you say 'to stop / end'?
How do you say 'to connect / plug in'?
How do you use 'se laisser + infinitive'?
cards.back: se laisser + infinitif = let / allow oneself to be or do something

cards.front: How do you say 'right away / immediately'?
How do you say 'very proud' with an idiom?
How do you say 'this allows to...'?
How do you say 'a book / written work'?
How do you say 'inconceivable / impossible to imagine'?
Which preposition do you use with jouer for an instrument?
cards.notes: Examples: jouer du piano; jouer de la batterie.

cards.front: Which preposition do you use with jouer for a sport/game?
How do you say 'to play on words'?
Unclear page 8 card: the note seems to pair this French verb with 'is forgotten'.
cards.notes: Handwritten translation is unclear; review the original page.

cards.back: parce que, car, puisque, à cause de, grâce à
donc, alors, c'est pourquoi, du coup, si bien que, tellement ... que

cards.front: Which indefinite words in the notes are pronouns?
Which indefinite words in the notes are adjectives?
cards.notes: chaque = every; quelques = some; aucun = no / not any.

cards.front: Which relative pronoun replaces the subject?
Which relative pronoun replaces a direct object?
Which relative pronoun indicates a place/link?

cards.notes: The note says dont replaces de + quelque chose.

cards.front: How do you form the gérondif?
cards.notes: Take the nous form, remove -ons, add -ant.

cards.front: What are examples of regular gérondif forms?
cards.back: parler → en parlant; finir → en finissant

cards.front: What are the irregular gérondif forms in the notes?
cards.back: avoir → en ayant; être → en étant; savoir → en sachant

cards.front: How do you say 'He walks while singing'?
How do you say 'I saw a man singing in the street'?
cards.back: J'ai vu un homme chantant dans la rue.
cards.notes: Here chantant is a present participle without en.

cards.front: Where do nationality, color, taste, appearance, and style adjectives usually go?
cards.notes: Examples from the notes include un gâteau sucré and une église baroque.

cards.front: Where do long adjectives, adjectives with complements, participles, and time adjectives usually go?
cards.notes: Examples: magnifique, difficile à ouvrir, déchiré, prochain/suivant.

cards.front: Which types of adjectives can go before the noun?
cards.notes: Examples in the notes: grand, gentil, belle, jeune, gros, premier, dernier, deuxième.

cards.front: What is the difference between 'ma propre maison' and 'ma maison propre'?
cards.back: ma propre maison = my own house; ma maison propre = my clean house

cards.front: What is the difference between 'un grand homme' and 'un homme grand'?
cards.back: un grand homme = a great man; un homme grand = a tall man

cards.front: How do you say 'to cry / complain' informally?
How do you say 'with long sleeves'?
What does the suffix -able often mean?
cards.back: -able = qui peut être fait / can be done

cards.front: Which -able spellings were corrected in the notes?
How do you say 'to take root'?
How do you say 'the next right'?
How do you say 'at the end of the street'?
How do you say 'weight training / workout'?
What is an informal abbreviation for 'meeting'?
Unclear slang verb on page 14 meaning 'to eat'?
cards.notes: The handwriting looks like grailler; review if needed.

cards.front: How do you say 'a news item / miscellaneous news story'?
How do you say 'to recover / collect'?
How do you say 'to draft / write'?
How do you say 'work of art'?
How do you say 'in the middle of the night / late at night'?
How do you say 'police officer / gendarme'?
cards.notes: In France, a gendarme is a member of the gendarmerie.
The handwritten form looks like sauve; infinitive is sauver.

cards.front: Unclear note for 'few / little' on page 16?
cards.notes: The handwriting may say en peu; review the original.
The note glosses it with restraining/limiting.

cards.front: How do you say 'to notice / catch sight of'?
How do you say 'to happen / take place'?
cards.notes: The page lists se passer without a clear English gloss.

cards.front: How do you say 'to rebel / revolt'?
How do you say 'an attempt / try'?
How do you say 'to lead / direct'?
